qualifying_org_definition: Agency or organization providing scheduled and structured unpaid volunteer work (Wyoming DFS SNAP/POWER Policy Manual, CM 700-series, Section 708(D)(1)(c); confirmed verbatim in Wyoming DFS legislative brief on SNAP Work Requirements, September 2019). No explicit 501(c)(3) restriction stated; broad 'agency or organization' language used.
verification_instrument: No Wyoming-specific volunteer verification form identified. Policy manual (CM 700) states that when an ABAWD claims 80 hours of work/volunteer activity, the caseworker should 'attempt to obtain as much verification as possible' based on the client's statement — consistent with self-attestation plus third-party documentation (e.g., a letter or timesheet from the volunteer organization). No named state form for volunteer hour verification found across multiple source searches, including the DFS forms library which lists only employment/income verification forms (DFS106, DFS108b, DFS109, DFS111).
hours_formula_detail: Wyoming follows the federal default: 80 hours/month (or 20 hours/week averaged monthly). Confirmed explicitly in the 2019 DFS legislative brief and the 2025 DFS program overview. No workfare-formula deviation found.
accepts_outofstate_detail: Wyoming DFS policy uses the phrase 'agency or organization' with no geographic restriction on the certifying entity. No source — including the CM 700 policy manual accordion, the 2019 DFS legislative brief, the 2025 program overview, or the FNS State Options Report — imposes an in-state requirement on the qualifying volunteer organization. Under the federal default (7 CFR 273.24), any nonprofit/organization providing structured unpaid volunteer work qualifies. Absent a state restriction, a CA-based 501(c)(3) such as Tended should be accepted operationally. No Wyoming DFS guidance addressing out-of-state nonprofits was found.
geographic_waivers: none — fully enforced statewide. Wyoming has never held an active ABAWD time limit waiver (confirmed in FNS State Options Report 16th Edition and corroborated by the OBBBA implementation guidance). As of 2026 all prior waivers nationally either expired or were terminated by November 2, 2026; Wyoming had none to begin with.
submission_deadline: Within 10 days from the end of the calendar month in which the ABAWD's hours decreased below threshold. Hours are verified on a monthly averaged basis; changes reportable within 10 days of month-end.
notes:
Wyoming DFS policy confirms unpaid volunteer work that is 'scheduled and structured' counts toward the 80-hour/month ABAWD requirement, and no mandatory prestep (EDP, county assignment, provider registration, MOU) was found in available sources. However, the Wyoming SNAP/POWER Policy Manual is not publicly browsable in full text — the DFS website routes to Google Drive folders requiring authentication — so the specific verification instrument and any state-imposed conditions could not be confirmed. Rated Tier 3 (blocked or unclear) due to inability to confirm the complete verification pathway; the federal default general volunteer lane likely applies, but a follow-up direct inquiry to Wyoming DFS (307-473-3948) is recommended before relying on Tier 1 status.

REFINEMENT PASS (2026-06-21): Adversarial re-examination substantially resolved the Tier 3 uncertainty. The CM-700 accordion page (https://dfs.wyo.gov/accordions/snap-and-power-policy-manual-700-extended-menu/) was successfully fetched and contains direct policy text: Section 708(D)(1)(c) states ABAWD work includes 'unpaid volunteer work with an agency or organization which is scheduled and structured,' and no mandatory registration, EDP, or IRP prestep is imposed on the SNAP ABAWD volunteer track. The DFS 568 Individual Responsibility Plan (IRP) referenced in the CM 700 series is confirmed to be POWER (TANF)-only; the manual explicitly states the DFS 568 referral is under the POWER section heading only, not SNAP ABAWD. Four adversarial searches were conducted: (1) WY SNAP E&T FY2026 state plan volunteer community service — found E&T page but no gating requirement; (2) WY SNAP ABAWD work registration intake case manager employment plan — found IRP is TANF-only; (3) WY food assistance ABAWD volunteer site approved list nonprofit verification form — no approved list found; (4) WY DFS SNAP POWER CM 700 ABAWD volunteer verification instrument — confirmed policy text but no named state verification form. The forms library (snap-how-to-apply-and-frequently-used-forms) lists only DFS106 (Employer Statement), DFS108b (Self Employment Verification), DFS109 (Rental Verification), DFS111 (Student Aid Worksheet) — no volunteer-specific form. Policy language on verification is 'attempt to obtain as much verification as possible' per client's statement, consistent with self-reported/self-attested model. Tier upgraded from 3 to 1_clean. Confidence set to medium rather than high because: (a) the full policy manual text remains in a Google Drive folder behind authentication so a complete section-by-section review was not possible; (b) the specific verification instrument form name was not confirmed; (c) no direct cite to a WY administrative rule (as opposed to the online policy accordion) was located. No evidence of any blocking condition — approved site lists, mandatory EDP, MOU requirement, or county-imposed gating — was found across all searches.

FINAL PASS (2026-06-21): Two primary source PDFs were successfully downloaded and extracted via pypdf. (1) Wyoming DFS/Economic Security Division, 'SNAP Work Requirements' (September 2019), a legislative brief prepared for the Joint Interim Committee — this DFS-authored document explicitly sets out in Table 2 that ABAWD qualifying work includes 'unpaid volunteer work with an agency or organization which is scheduled and structured,' with zero mention of any required prestep, approved site list, EDP, or special intake. The full document was read; no hidden gating condition exists. (2) Wyoming DFS/Economic Security, 'Program Overview: Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program' (November 2025) — confirms ABAWD must meet 80 hours/month or qualifying E&T, no volunteer-specific conditions or gating mentioned. Additionally, the USDA FNS SNAP State Options Report (16th Edition, current as of October 1, 2023) Wyoming state profile (PDF page 84) was extracted and read directly; it confirms: 'Voluntary and Mandatory SNAP E&T Programs: Voluntary only' (ruling out any mandatory E&T enrollment prestep), 'Program Administration: State administered' (confirming no county-level variation), 'ABAWD Time Limit Waiver: No ABAWD time limit waiver' (confirming enforcement), 'ABAWD Discretionary Exemptions: Use of discretionary exemptions.' The CM Updates page confirms no updates to Section 708 volunteer lane since a 2018 clarification. All four independent source types — (a) DFS-authored legislative brief PDF, (b) DFS program overview PDF, (c) DFS online policy accordion, (d) FNS federal state options report — are consistent: open volunteer lane, no prestep, self-reported hours, no named state form, voluntary-only E&T. Adversarial search found no blocking condition across all tested angles. Confidence elevated to HIGH per the refined rubric: general_volunteer_lane confirmed, required_prestep confirmed none, hours_reporting confirmed self_reported, 4 independent sources agree, primary policy source text (DFS legislative brief, FNS state options) was successfully read and confirms lane shape, no contradicting evidence found.

EXTENDED FIELDS PASS (2026-06-21): Five new dimensions added. (1) Hours formula: WY follows federal 80-hour/month standard (federal_80); confirmed by DFS 2019 legislative brief, 2025 program overview, and FNS State Options Report — no workfare-formula deviation. (2) Out-of-state nonprofit: policy uses broad 'agency or organization' with no geographic restriction; no source imposes an in-state requirement; rated 'silent' — under the federal default an out-of-state 501(c)(3) like Tended should be accepted operationally. (3) Geographic waivers: none — Wyoming never held an ABAWD waiver; all national waivers expired or terminated by November 2, 2026 under OBBBA; fully enforced statewide. (4) Submission UX: DFS accepts SNAP-related submissions by paper at county office, mail, fax, or email (snappowerservice@wyo.gov); reporting is monthly with a 10-day deadline from end of calendar month when hours decrease; cadence rated 'monthly'. (5) Form required fields: no named WY state volunteer verification form exists; default baseline fields applied (org_name, org_signature, supervisor_name, recipient_name, hours_total, activity_description, period_start_date, period_end_date) consistent with a standard letter/timesheet format.
